# Transcrição: 04- How to Add Pages, People & Ad Accounts 

- **Fonte:** 04- How to Add Pages, People & Ad Accounts 
- **Duração:** 09:48
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

In this training we're going to go over how to add people, how to add pages, and how to add ad accounts to your business manager. So if you don't know how to get to the business manager, you need to go to the previous training and it'll walk you through exactly how to get to the business manager and also how to set up the business manager. So if you want to add contractors or team members to your business manager so that they can view certain assets or edit certain assets or if you have contractors so that they can manage certain ad accounts, you're going to want to go to the people tab here and then you want to click add to add the person. And then you're going to be prompted here to enter an email address and then also differentiate which kind of access you want to give them. So initially you want to enter that email address or Derek at my favorite ad agency.com. Hopefully that's not a real email address. And then essentially you want to assign the business role that you want to give them. So this is for your whole business manager. So you want to designate if you want to give them employee access or if you want to give them admin access. So employee access is what we typically recommend when adding employees and virtually anyone else just so that they can see the ad accounts and the tools that they're assigned to. Admin access, you're going to be able to have control over your entire business. So they're going to be able to edit your settings, the people who are added, the accounts, the tools, as well as the credit card information. So it's really important to designate what kind of access you want to give people because you don't want to give them control to things that you don't want them to see or touch. You can also assign these new roles as a financial analyst or a financial editor. Basically the financial analyst is going to be able to view the business financial details like the transactions, the invoices, the account spend, and the payment methods. So this might be good to add in an accountant or someone on your team who is dealing with all the finances. And then the finance editor, this is just someone who can edit the credit card information and financial details. So this is really important as well. If you have someone specifically on your team that is managing that, managing the account spend, managing the payment methods, and the invoices, then these are great tools to use for that. So once you've decided this, you're just going to click next. And that's going to direct you to this page where it's going to ask you which pages you want to grant them access to. So since this is on your business manager, all of the pages that your business manager has access to, and has admin access to, you're going to be able to grant them a degree of access and a degree of permission. So if you're just yourself, or if you have a business here, you're just going to have your own business page. If you have an agency and you have multiple pages that you're managing, you're going to have that whole list here as well. So here we just have test page. So we're going to use test page. And then you'll be prompted to set the permissions

## [03:02 - 06:04]

that you want that person to have. So here you're going to be basically given the choice to give standard access or admin access. So standard access is basically going to cover things from published content to view page performance. So you can manage really everything down to what you want them to see and what you want them to be able to edit here. So if you want them to just publish content, they're going to be able to send messages and publish as the page. So this is pretty key, they're not going to be publishing as themselves, or they will have the option to also publish as the page. So the next step is moderate comments. So they're going to be able to moderate comments as your page as well as as themselves. You're not going to be able to send messages as this, and you're not going to be able to publish as the page, but you're going to be able to moderate the comments as your page. The next step is creating ads. So you're going to be able to grant them access to advertise for your page. The final one is view performance. So basically this is the lowest access that you can give. You're just, they're just going to be able to see the analytics, the page insights, and about the page. So if you grant publish content, you're going to be giving them access to everything and so on and so on. So if you give them moderate comments, you're going to be giving them access to create ads as well as view page performance. If you just want them to view that page performance, then you just click one of them. But as you can see, if you add publish content, that'll show up for every single one of them. So the next step is admin access. So if you grant someone admin access, they're going to be able to basically perform any action on your page. So view all the page activity, view the performance, manage page roles, and then they're also going to be able to manage the payment information as well. So this is really, really important and something you really want to consider as you're setting these access levels. So we're going to just grant access to these for now and invite. And basically it's going to send them an email and it's going to show up here. So you can add more people if you want, but essentially it's going to send them an email. So it's going to send Derek at myfavoriteagency.com and then you click done here. And then essentially it's going to be pending here until that person accepts. And once they accept, they're going to have access to all of the assets that you gave them access to. So unless you own the page that you're looking to get access to, you're going to want to request access to that page. So in order to do that, you add, you click add here when you're in the pages tab. And you just click, you don't want to click add a page because if you click add a page, this is something that you own. You want to request access to that page. So this is if you're managing a client page or if you're managing a page for someone else, you want to click request access to a page. And then essentially you're going to want to type in that page name or URL. So we're going to use eight loop social again. And then you're going to be prompted to select what kind of access you're going to need.

## [06:04 - 09:08]

So this is going to be sent to the client or sent to the person who owns the page. And they're going to be given the opportunity to accept or decline this. So if you're just doing an audit, you might want to just do view page performance. If you want to actually create ads and if you are onboarding this client, you want to be able to do everything. So you want to select all of those. And then if you really need admin access, you can also select that here too. And then essentially just request access, you send that off and that person will be prompted to approve your access. Now if you're looking to add an ad account or gain access to an ad account, you want to go to the ad accounts tab and you want to click the blue button that says add. And again here, you want to make sure that you're not adding the ad account. So you're not saying you own the ad account. This is actually claiming it into business manager. You want to just request access. So again, request access, you want to type in the ad account ID. So that's the ad account ID. And then you want to be able to manage the campaigns and view the performance. So this is similar to how it used to be set up in the sense of analyst access. So this is kind of analyst access. If you just need to see what's going on and you want to do an audit of the account versus here where you're actually more of an advertiser and you're able to manage the campaigns, create and edit the ads, access reports and view all the ads. So if you just need those, select that. If you do need admin access, you can control and manage the entire ad account. So you can control the finances, the permissions, you can create and access and edit the ads. And then you can also access reports and view all the ads that are running or have been run. And then you just click confirm and that will request access to that ad account. Another cool feature that they have recently released is hiding closed ad accounts. So in the past, what used to happen is if you had a closed ad account, you could never unsee it. It would always show up in your tabs, but now you can toggle this little button here and you can basically have it so that it doesn't show any of the closed ad accounts, which is actually really awesome. It just makes things a lot cleaner. You can also filter this now by advertising for yourself, advertising for a partner owned by yourself, owned by a partner, active, disabled or closed. So this is really cool, especially if you're managing a whole ton of ad accounts. You can also sort by name ascending, name descending and most recently created. This is definitely a big deal, especially if you're dealing with a ton of different ad accounts. So another cool feature you can use if you're an agency and maybe you're hiring on a white label agency or something like that to work with is you can actually add partners in here as well. So you can add people based on their business ID. So essentially you want to add them in so that they can access assets that your business owns. So while they have access to this, your business can still be in control of the asset, but

## [09:08 - 09:48]

essentially it's going to give them access to certain pages and you will grant which pages you want to give them access to as well. So here for example, if you add a business ID in, click next, you're going to be prompted to assign assets based on the asset type here and essentially you're going to be able to give them the same kind of permissions as you would a page. So this is great if you're an agency and you're looking to hire on a white label agency and they might want to grant access to specific business assets within that as well. So if you're working with another agency and if that agency is also wanting to assign various different assets to different members on their team.
